content.claim: Teachers in Finland's tiered support system reported positive overall assessment but identified challenges including extended documentation burdens, insufficient time to support all pupils in mixed classes, and in some municipalities, cost-saving motives driving tier assignment rather than pedagogical rationale.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Teacher survey data reflect practitioner perception rather than objective measurement of workload or instructional time loss
Cost-saving motive claim is a union allegation not independently verified across cited sources
No quantitative data on actual documentation time or class composition effects on student outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
